ksa_text: Define, document, and implement enterprise metric definitions in the semantic layer, ensuring consistency across BI tools, business intelligence platforms, and analytical consumers through metric governance.

dcwf.top_element_label: Utilize data structures to organize, sort, and manipulate elements of information

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Architecture & Analytics is a primary or secondary domain for the Domain Data Lead. This task KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (team lead).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Data Architecture & Analytics), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.423). External validation of this task KSA for the Domain Data Lead role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: DAMA-DMBOK, analytics maturity models (Gartner, Forrester), and analytics leadership surveys confirm data architecture and analytics competencies for senior roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of Data Architecture & Analytics for the Domain Data Lead is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: Data Architecture & Analytics task KSA is within scope for the Domain Data Lead at the team lead tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
